name: Ermita de San Marcos, Peña Isasa, Cueva del Vico – circular route
name_original: Ermita de San Marcos, Peña Isasa, Cueva del Vico – ruta circular
region_intro: Hiking trails around Arnedo offer a diverse landscape for outdoor enthusiasts, situated in the Cidacos River valley in La Rioja, Spain. The region features a mix of accessible greenways, such as the Greenway of the Cidacos, and more challenging routes through natural parks. Hikers can explore areas characterized by river valleys, rock formations like Peña Isasa, and the foothills of mountain ranges such as the Sierra de Cebollera. This varied terrain provides options for different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Arnedo The most popular hiking route is Ermita de San Marcos, Peña Isasa, Cueva del Vico – circular route, a 9.8 miles (15.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 27 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers expansive views from Peña Isasa. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peña Isasa Loop from Palomares, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.3 km) path. This trail navigates varied terrain around the prominent Peña Isasa rock formation. Local hikers also love the Mirador de San Marcos — circular desde Arnedo, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail leading through scenic landscapes to a viewpoint, often completed in about 1 hour 49 minutes. Hiking around Arnedo is defined by river valleys, former railway greenways, and distinctive rock formations like Peña Isasa. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Arnedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 60 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Arnedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Arnedo, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: Arnedo offers a diverse selection of over 20 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with 6 easy trails perfect for a relaxed stroll, 15 moderate routes for those seeking a bit more challenge, and 2 difficult trails for experienced hikers looking for a strenuous ascent.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Arnedo?
A: The best time for hiking in Arnedo is typically during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making it ideal for exploring the region's diverse landscapes, from river valleys to rock formations. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder temperatures, though hiking is possible year-round.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options suitable for children in Arnedo?
A: Yes, Arnedo provides several family-friendly hiking options, particularly along its Greenways (Vías Verdes). These former railway lines, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, offer accessible and relatively flat paths, perfect for walks with children. There are 6 easy trails in total, providing gentle routes for families to enjoy the natural surroundings.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Arnedo?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in the Arnedo area, especially on the Greenways and in natural outdoor spaces. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock, and to carry waste bags to clean up after them. Always check local signage for specific restrictions.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available near Arnedo?
A: Yes, Arnedo features several excellent circular hiking routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1200929367/mirador-de-san-marcos-circular-desde-arnedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de San Marcos — circular desde Arnedo&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.8-mile (6.1 km) trail that leads to a scenic viewpoint. For a more challenging option, consider the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989470466/ermita-de-san-marcos-pena-isasa-cueva-del-vico-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Marcos, Peña Isasa, Cueva del Vico – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which offers expansive views from Peña Isasa.

Q: What natural landmarks or attractions can I see while hiking around Arnedo?
A: The Arnedo region is rich in natural beauty and landmarks. Hikers can encounter prominent rock formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning views. The area also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, following the river, and unique geological formations such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6307839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red Cliffs of Herce&lt;/a&gt;. You might also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6307877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas del Ajedrezado of Santa Eulalia Somera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural points of interest near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond its natural landscapes, Arnedo and its surroundings offer historical insights. You can visit the medieval castle in Arnedo, reflecting Muslim architecture. Near some trails, you might find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336999" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Marcos&lt;/a&gt;, a religious building, or even the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5344963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Préjano Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The region's history is intertwined with its natural setting, providing a unique cultural dimension to your outdoor visit.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Arnedo?
A: The hiking trails around Arnedo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, which includes river valleys, accessible greenways, and distinctive rock formations like Peña Isasa. Many appreciate the variety of routes catering to different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents, and the expansive views from higher points.

Q: Are there any trails that offer particularly good views?
A: For breathtaking views, trails that ascend to or around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/a&gt; are highly recommended. The difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989470466/ermita-de-san-marcos-pena-isasa-cueva-del-vico-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Marcos, Peña Isasa, Cueva del Vico – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is known for its expansive vistas. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1200929367/mirador-de-san-marcos-circular-desde-arnedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de San Marcos — circular desde Arnedo&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic landscapes leading to a rewarding viewpoint.

Q: What are the options for parking near the hiking trails in Arnedo?
A: Parking availability varies depending on the specific trailhead. For routes starting directly from Arnedo, you can often find street parking or designated parking areas within the town. For trails located further afield, such as those near smaller villages or natural parks, there are usually small parking lots or roadside spaces available. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for recommended starting points and parking information.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers seeking a challenge, Arnedo offers more demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989470466/ermita-de-san-marcos-pena-isasa-cueva-del-vico-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Marcos, Peña Isasa, Cueva del Vico – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult, covering 9.8 miles (15.7 km) with significant elevation gain. These routes often venture into areas like the foothills of the Sierra de Cebollera, providing strenuous ascents and rewarding views.

Q: Can I access hiking trails around Arnedo using public transport?
A: While some trails might be accessible from Arnedo town center, which has public transport connections, many of the more remote trailheads may require private vehicle access. The Greenways, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, often connect towns and villages, potentially offering more public transport options for reaching starting points. For specific routes, it's best to research local bus schedules to nearby villages.

Q: Are there any trails that follow river valleys or offer waterside views?
A: Yes, the region's landscape is defined by the Cidacos River valley. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of the Cidacos&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option for waterside walks, as it follows the river, offering accessible paths and views of the river itself and its surrounding natural beauty. This provides a pleasant contrast to the more rugged mountain trails.
